note: This module has no plugins, entities, or Drush commands. Its only introspectable/buildable state is one simple-config object, layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings, with two sequence keys: layout_builder_iframe_routes (the 10 built-in Layout Builder routes that open in the admin-themed iframe modal, all enabled by default) and custom_routes (extra opt-in dialog routes, empty by default). All medium/hard cases are therefore grounded in these two route lists — the same values the settings form at /admin/config/content/layout_builder_iframe_modal writes. Easy cases are response-graded facts (config object + keys, settings route/path, permission, dependency).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Layout Builder iFrame Modal (layout_builder_iframe_modal) module enabled, which single config object stores its settings, and what are the two keys inside it that control which routes open in the iframe modal?
evals.expected_output: All behavior is driven by the simple-config object layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings, which has two sequence keys: layout_builder_iframe_routes (the built-in Layout Builder routes to iframe) and custom_routes (extra opt-in dialog routes).

evals.prompt: The Layout Builder iFrame Modal module adds an admin settings form to choose which Layout Builder routes render in the iframe modal. What is its route name and URL path?
evals.expected_output: The settings form is route layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings at /admin/config/content/layout_builder_iframe_modal (under Configuration > Content authoring).

evals.prompt: I want to install drupal/layout_builder_iframe_modal on a Drupal 11 site and control who can change its route settings. Which Drupal module does it depend on, and which permission gates its settings form?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the core layout_builder module. Its settings form is gated by the permission 'configure layout builder iframe modal'.

evals.prompt: On this live Drupal 11 site, Layout Builder iFrame Modal has been configured with an extra custom route opted into the iframe modal. Inspect the site and tell me which custom route(s) are currently set in its custom_routes list.
evals.expected_output: Reading layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings (drush config:get layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings custom_routes) shows custom_routes contains 'my_module.special_dialog'.

evals.prompt: A site owner says the Layout Builder 'Add block' form still opens in the old off-canvas tray instead of the iframe modal on this site, even though the module is enabled. Inspect the live configuration: is the layout_builder.add_block route currently enabled in the module's iframe route list, and which config key controls it?
evals.expected_output: The key layout_builder_iframe_routes in layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings controls it. It currently lists the other 9 built-in routes but NOT layout_builder.add_block, so Add block is not set to open in the iframe modal.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site, configure Layout Builder iFrame Modal so that the route entity.node.edit_form is added to its custom_routes list (opting that route into the iframe modal), without removing any of the built-in Layout Builder routes. Persist the change to the layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings config on the live site.

On this Drupal 11 site, configure Layout Builder iFrame Modal so that ONLY the 'Add block' and 'Update block' Layout Builder routes open in the iframe modal, and no other built-in Layout Builder route does. Save the setting so layout_builder_iframe_routes in layout_builder_iframe_modal.settings holds exactly those two routes on the live site.
